canonical_filename.mechanism: SKILL.md (required, fixed filename for directory-form skills)

compatibility.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: compatibility (optional, max 500 chars)

custom_filename.mechanism: Any .md filename at root of ~/.pi/agent/skills/ or .pi/skills/ (shorthand single-file form)

description.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: description (required, max 1024 chars)

disable_model_invocation.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: disable-model-invocation (optional bool); confirmed in SkillFrontmatter TypeScript interface as disable-model-invocation?: boolean

display_name.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: name (required, max 64 chars, lowercase a-z/0-9/hyphens, must match parent directory name)

metadata_map.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: metadata (optional, arbitrary key-value mapping)

providerExtensions.name: allowed-tools frontmatter field
providerExtensions.description: Space-delimited list of pre-approved tools (experimental). Pre-approves tool access for the skill without user confirmation prompt.

providerExtensions.name: Supporting files in skill directory
providerExtensions.description: Skill directories may include arbitrary supporting files organized by convention into scripts/ (helper scripts), references/ (detailed docs loaded on-demand), and assets/ (templates, data files). The agent resolves relative paths in SKILL.md against the skill directory and uses them in tool commands.

providerExtensions.name: Description-based auto-invocation
providerExtensions.description: At startup pi scans skill locations and injects names and descriptions into the system prompt in XML format. When a task matches a skill description, the agent uses the read tool to load the full SKILL.md on-demand (progressive disclosure). Users can force invocation via /skill:name commands.

providerExtensions.name: Cross-harness skill loading via settings array
providerExtensions.description: Pi's settings skills array accepts paths to other providers' skill directories (e.g., ~/.claude/skills, ~/.codex/skills). This allows a pi session to load and use skills authored for Claude Code or OpenAI Codex without any conversion. No other known provider loads other providers' skill directories.

providerExtensions.name: Package.json and monorepo skill discovery
providerExtensions.description: Skills can be declared via pi.skills entries in package.json or placed in skills/ subdirectories within npm packages. This enables monorepo skill discovery: each package can ship its own skills, discovered automatically when pi runs in that package's directory.

providerExtensions.name: CLI flags for runtime skill management
providerExtensions.description: --skill <path> (repeatable) loads additional skill files or directories at runtime; additive even when --no-skills is set. --no-skills disables discovery of all default skill locations, leaving only paths provided via --skill.

providerExtensions.name: Ignore-file respect during skill scanning
providerExtensions.description: Pi respects .gitignore, .ignore, and .fdignore files when scanning skill directories. Patterns are read from each subdirectory and prefixed with the relative path so they apply correctly to nested content. Implemented in the TypeScript scanner via the ignore npm package.
